texto: Stories are like DNA, they shape the culture that they’re a part of. A society is not a society without its own unique stories. But we allow machines to make our stories, nowadays, or at least to tell them. We allow things to shape our understanding of who we are. We are entertained, not nurtured. We are given Twinkies for our mind, things that amuse but do not enlighten. It tickles our taste buds, but it does not enrich us.

texto: My problem as a writer is that—whenever I meet someone for the first time—I immediately invent for them a personality and background that are invariably more interesting than the ones they possess. texto: Once you realize that life and love are not things you can possess but forces that emanate through us and through all creation, then you can begin to appreciate the miracle you are a part of. seo.descriptionSeo: Citação de James Rozoff sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Feminism is not inherently bourgeois, but bourgeois feminism is the only one given voice in our s…” a…

frase_original: Feminism is not inherently bourgeois, but bourgeois feminism is the only one given voice in our society. That is why women’s issues discuss glass ceilings rather than dirt floors.

texto: Intelligence resides between us as much as it resides in us. And it resides within the subconscious much more than it does the conscious. We’re just conscious-biased: our conscious mind wants us to think it’s the be all and end all.

texto: It is wisdom to know who you are, but not to the point of excluding who you might become.
explicacao: Citação de James Rozoff sobre temas ligados a sabedoria. “It is wisdom to know who you are, but not to the point of excluding who you might become.” articula sabedoria, potential, self-knowing em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como self-knowing e self-knowledge.
